query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "qwen-vla: unifying vision-language-action modeling across tasks, envir" computer science
rationale: This paper belongs in the literature review because it addresses the same core mechanism (Vision-Language-Action modeling) and domain (robotic manipulation) as the user's question, specifically extending the canonical VLA framework to handle adverse sensing conditions (darkness, blur) which is a critical sub-problem for "unifying VLA across tasks and environments."
